phone_presence_notes: Polite and professional with consistent 'yes sir' language, but purely transactional — no rapport-building or name use.
top_strength: Clear and accurate pricing explanation — covered hourly rate, 2.5hr minimum, total estimate, and what the clock includes vs. excludes without being prompted.
coaching_note: Zero on Set the Agenda and Save Your Ass are the biggest gaps. Dustin needs an opener that frames the call and builds value before pricing, and must add the consolidation/prep speech — especially on a minimal-item move where the customer is already questioning the minimum. No differentiators were mentioned at all (damage coverage, Meet Your Mover, On-Time Guarantee) — customer had competing quotes and got nothing to justify Einstein's price.
